items.q: What happens when 11111111 + 1 is computed in an 8-bit register?
items.correct: Overflow - the result requires 9 bits but only 8 are available.
items.why: Students miss that registers have fixed width. The mathematical result is 9 bits, but the 8-bit register drops the carry-out, producing 00000000 with an overflow flag.

items.q: What is the denary value of binary 10110?
items.why: Students forget to apply place values. Each position is a power of 2 from right (1, 2, 4, 8, 16...). Reading binary as denary is the most common error.

items.q: Which item is removed first from a stack?
items.why: Students confuse stacks (LIFO - like a stack of plates) with queues (FIFO - like a checkout line). The 'stack of plates' analogy makes LIFO concrete.
The answer examiners want is: To credit and avoid plagiarism. “To make work longer” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: An important word in a search
items.why: The answer examiners want is: An important word in a search. “A computer chip” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many sources should you check at minimum to confirm a fact?
items.why: Exact answer: 2. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
